title: The First Sunday of Advent
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: November 27, 2022

## LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The First Sunday of Advent marks the beginning of the church year in the Christian calendar, a season of spiritual preparation and expectation leading toward Christmas. Under the Revised Common Lectionary (RCL), the readings for this date would have included Isaiah 64:1-9, Psalm 80:1-7,16-18, 1 Corinthians 1:3-9, and Mark 13:24-37—passages emphasizing Jesus's return, watchfulness, and divine restoration. Advent in 2022 was particularly notable as it began just four weeks before Christmas, making this an unusually compressed season that year.

## EPISCOPAL/ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church had recently emerged from significant internal tensions surrounding LGBTQ+ inclusion and biblical interpretation that dominated the 2022 General Convention in Baltimore (held in July 2022). The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Glenna Huber (who began her tenure in 2020), was navigating these broader denominational conversations while serving the state's growing and increasingly diverse urban centers. No major diocesan leadership changes or crises were occurring in late November 2022, though the church continued working through implementation of General Convention resolutions on marriage equality and pastoral care.

## DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing unseasonably warm weather for late November, with temperatures in the mid-50s Fahrenheit—unusual for approaching winter. The Colorado state legislature was in interim period (not in session), though local city council and county commissioners were addressing affordable housing shortages and homelessness, particularly visible in downtown Denver and Capitol Hill near the cathedral. The Denver Broncos football team was in the midst of a disappointing 2022 NFL season, currently sitting at 3-7 record, a source of local frustration.

## NATIONAL NEWS

The midterm elections had concluded just three weeks prior (November 8), with Republicans gaining House control but Democrats retaining Senate control—a better-than-expected result for the party in power. Inflation remained at elevated levels (around 7.7% year-over-year), a dominant concern affecting American households heading into the holiday season. The Astroworld Festival crowd surge tragedy aftermath continued reverberating, with legal and regulatory discussions ongoing about concert safety and artist responsibility.

## INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The war in Ukraine was in its ninth month, with Russian forces recently abandoning Kherson (Ukraine's regional capital they had occupied) in early November, marking a significant Ukrainian military success. The COP27 climate conference had just concluded in Egypt (November 20), establishing a "loss and damage" fund for climate-vulnerable nations but with limited specifics on implementation. Iran was experiencing ongoing protests following the September death of Mahsa Amini in police custody, with university students and women leading demonstrations against the government.

## CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in the liminal space between election exhaustion and holiday anticipation, with families beginning travel and shopping for Christmas while the nation remained deeply polarized. The cultural conversation was shifting toward end-of-year reckonings and resolutions, with streaming platforms releasing awards-season contenders and holiday specials. Denver's Capitol Hill neighborhood, historically bohemian and progressive, was experiencing gentrification pressures and demographic shifts—creating a mixed-income congregation environment where economic anxiety and hope for renewal (fitting for Advent theology) would have resonated with diverse congregants.
